_comment: Battle AI phase 2 subcommander demo (#585): mutual support. Freshly authored for the review round that fixed UnitLeader.decide's own priority gap (a subcommander directive must never interrupt a unit already chasing a live, still-fighting target -- see UnitLeader.is_chasing_live_target) -- not copied from any other PR's scripted-input file (see .claude/memories/sparta-demos.md's 'author each demo scenario fresh' entry). The fix means the standard 5v5 line spawn can no longer demonstrate mutual support at all: with omniscient, INF-range targeting, every team-1 unit picks up a live ATTACK order on the very first AI tick (tick 0, before any physics has run -- Battle._physics_process runs _run_enemy_ai() before the Units' own _physics_process), so none of them is ever 'available' for a directive to redirect. This scenario stages the one case that stays reachable: team 1's Infantry (uid 2, 'ward') is paired against a full-strength team-0 Infantry it fights for a long time, while team 1's Cavalry (uid 3, 'ally') is paired against a token 1-soldier team-0 unit that dies almost immediately -- freeing the Cavalry (is_chasing_live_target reads false once its own target is dead) while the Infantry is still fighting. Verified by state dump (tools/demo/dump-state.sh): the Cavalry's current_order reads SUPPORT (guarding the Infantry) by tick 120, then it closes the distance and joins the fight directly. Camera opens on a wide establishing view covering both initial pairings (so the token unit's quick death and the Cavalry's idle window are both visible), holds through the SUPPORT order landing, then eases into a close-up on the combined melee as the Cavalry arrives.
